title: helianthemum guttatum subsp. macrosepalum
intro: Helianthemum guttatum subsp. macrosepalum, also known as the rockrose, is a flowering plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Helianthemum guttatum subsp. macrosepalum include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-aging properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
